The '''SD-6 "Hulk" infantry droid''' was a predecessor to the [[Balmorran Arms/Legends|Balmorran Arms]] [[SD-9 battle droid|SD-9]] and [[SD-10 battle droid|SD-10]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] models, deployed during the last centuries of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and the early years of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:SD-6HulkInfantryDroid.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A SD-6 Hulk infantry droid]]
The SD-6 was a fourth-degree [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] manufactured by [[Balmorran Arms/Legends|Balmorran Arms]]. With a height of 3.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] and almost the same width, it was a veritable [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] of a defensive unit.<ref name="UA" /> The droid was armed with two [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web repeating blasters]] on each arm as well as two [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion gun rifles]] on rotating shoulder mounts. It was also equipped with two large arms to defend itself from any attack with melee weapons.<ref name="UA" />

Like its predecessors, the [[SD-4 battle droid|SD-4]] and [[SD-5 battle droid|SD-5]] models, the SD-6 series was equipped with data illegally acquired from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Dark Trooper Project]],<ref name="EGtD" /> but lacked a [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processor]]. It had a tendency to act single-mindedly, without being able to think independently.<ref name="GoA">{{DoD|The Gem of Alderaan}}</ref> The SD-6 had an integrated [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and a [[Color/Legends|blue]] or red [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]], which allowed for telescopic vision.<ref name="UA" /> The battle [[Droid/Legends|droid]] also sported [[Duranium/Legends|duranium]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]],<ref name="TotG" /> which was as thick as a [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] [[Hull/Legends|hull]] in some places and made it virtually immune to the fire of most personal weapons.<ref name="UA" />

The SD-6 functioned as a mobile, intelligent turret, protecting positions or laying down covering fire. The armament allowed the "Hulk" to lay down a barrage of fire, able to fend off both droid and organic infantry assaults. It was most effective against smaller vehicles and large groups of infantry. The droid's low speed, however, made it vulnerable to smaller teams of individuals and mobile infantry units, which could dodge the droid's arcs of fire.<ref name="UA" /> The SD-6 also worked well in tandem with regular infantry units in offensive<ref name="GoA" /> and defensive situations, or as a battle droid commander.<ref name="TotG" />

==History==
Developed during the last centuries of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the "Hulk" saw most of its action deployed by the Old Republic as a defensive unit for vulnerable hangar facilities and landing pads. The units provided cover fire for incoming starships and prevented hostile ships from taking off.<ref name="UA" /> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Republic initially withdrew the droids from active duty to avoid confusion with the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] (CIS).<ref name="TotG" /> Service and production later restarted with Balmorra producing SD-6s for sale to Republic ground forces alongside B1s meant for the CIS.<ref name="TEGtWAC12">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-12-tales-of-the-new-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 12: Tales of the New Republic}}</ref>

During the days of the Galactic Empire, the original duties of the SD-6 were taken over by smaller and more agile droids,<ref name="UA" /> but the Imperial armed forces still used the SD-6.<ref name="TotG" /> As the series was phased out, many units were either scrapped or sold.<ref name="UA" /> Although illegal, some [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] acquired these war droids to protect their personal starships or act as security forces—alone or supported by other minions.<ref name="TotG" /> [[Mercenary/Legends|Mercenaries]] also acquired several SD-6 war droids. [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] mercenaries used one during their [[Battle of the Gem of Alderaan|attempt]] to take over the [[Hovertrain/Legends|repulsortrain]] ''[[Gem of Alderaan]]'' to capture [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]].<ref name="GoA" />

Although also used by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],<ref name="TotG" /> the SD-6 was nearly nonexistent during that [[New Republic era|era]],<ref name="UA" /> as the [[SD-9 battle droid|SD-9]] and [[SD-10 battle droid|SD-10]] battle droids had been developed to take its place.<ref name="EGtD" /> The few remaining units that had not been melted down were left to their own devices and abandoned.<ref name="UA" />
